controls.title: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.description: Actively manage (inventory, track, and correct) all enterprise assets (end-user devices, including portable and mobile; network devices; non-computing/IoT devices; and servers) connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments, to accurately know the totality of assets that need to be monitored and protected within the enterprise.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain Detailed Enterprise Asset Inventory
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all enterprise assets with the potential to store or process data, to include: end-user devices (including portable and mobile), network devices, non-computing/IoT devices, and servers. Ensure the inventory captures the network address (if static), hardware address, machine name, enterprise asset owner, department for each asset, and whether the asset has been approved to connect to the network. For mobile end-user devices, MDM type tools can support this process, where appropriate. This inventory includes assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments. Additionally, it includes assets that are regularly connected to the enterprise's network infrastructure, even if they are not under control of the enterprise. Review and update the inventory of all enterprise assets bi-annually, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Address Unauthorized Assets
safeguards.description: Ensure that a process exists to address unauthorized assets on a weekly basis. The enterprise may choose to remove the asset from the network, deny the asset from connecting remotely to the network, or quarantine the asset.

safeguards.title: Utilize an Active Discovery Tool
safeguards.description: Utilize an active discovery tool to identify assets connected to the enterprise's network. Configure the active discovery tool to execute daily, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Use Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) Logging to Update Enterprise Asset Inventory
safeguards.description: Use DHCP logging on all DHCP servers or Internet Protocol (IP) address management tools to update the enterprise's asset inventory. Review and use logs to update the enterprise's asset inventory weekly, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Use a Passive Asset Discovery Tool
safeguards.description: Use a passive discovery tool to identify assets connected to the enterprise's network. Review and use scans to update the enterprise's asset inventory at least weekly, or more frequently.

controls.title: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
controls.description: Actively manage (inventory, track, and correct) all software (operating systems and applications) on the network so that only authorized software is installed and can execute, and that unauthorized and unmanaged software is found and prevented from installation or execution.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Software Inventory
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a detailed inventory of all licensed software installed on enterprise assets. The software inventory must document the title, publisher, initial install/use date, and business purpose for each entry; where appropriate, include the Uniform Resource Locator (URL), app store(s), version(s), deployment mechanism, and decommission date. Review and update the software inventory bi-annually, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Ensure Authorized Software is Currently Supported
safeguards.description: Ensure that only currently supported software is designated as authorized in the software inventory for enterprise assets. If software is unsupported yet necessary for the fulfillment of the enterprise's mission, document an exception detailing mitigating controls and residual risk acceptance. For any unsupported software without an exception documentation, designate as unauthorized. Review the software list to verify software support at least monthly, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Address Unauthorized Software
safeguards.description: Ensure that unauthorized software is either removed from use on enterprise assets or receives a documented exception. Review monthly, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Utilize Automated Software Inventory Tools
safeguards.description: Utilize software inventory tools throughout the enterprise to automate the discovery and documentation of installed software.

safeguards.title: Allowlist Authorized Software
safeguards.description: Use technical controls, such as application allowlisting, to ensure that only authorized software can execute or be accessed. Reassess bi-annually, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Allowlist Authorized Libraries
safeguards.description: Use technical controls to ensure that only authorized software libraries, such as specific .dll, .ocx, .so, etc. files, are allowed to load into a system process. Block unauthorized libraries from loading into a system process. Reassess bi-annually, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Allowlist Authorized Scripts
safeguards.description: Use technical controls, such as digital signatures and version control, to ensure that only authorized scripts, such as specific .ps1, .py, etc. files, are allowed to execute. Block unauthorized scripts from executing. Reassess bi-annually, or more frequently.

controls.title: Data Protection
controls.description: Develop processes and technical controls to identify, classify, securely handle, retain, and dispose of data.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Management Process
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a data management process. In the process, address data sensitivity, data owner, handling of data, data retention limits, and disposal requirements based on sensitivity and retention standards for the enterprise. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Inventory
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a data inventory based on the enterprise's data management process. Inventory sensitive data, at a minimum. Review and update inventory annually, at a minimum, with a priority on sensitive data.

safeguards.title: Configure Data Access Control Lists
safeguards.description: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

safeguards.title: Enforce Data Retention
safeguards.description: Retain data according to the enterprise's data management process. Data retention must include both minimum and maximum timelines.

safeguards.title: Securely Dispose of Data
safeguards.description: Securely dispose of data as outlined in the enterprise's data management process. Ensure the disposal process and method are commensurate with the data sensitivity.

safeguards.title: Encrypt Data on End-User Devices
safeguards.description: Encrypt data on end-user devices containing sensitive data. Example implementations can include: Windows BitLocker®, Apple FileVault®, Linux® dm-crypt.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Classification Scheme
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an overall data classification scheme for the enterprise. Enterprises may use labels, such as Sensitive, Confidential, and Public and classify data according to those labels. Review and update the classification scheme annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Document Data Flows
safeguards.description: Document data flows. Data flow documentation includes service provider data flows and should be based on the enterprise's data management process. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Encrypt Data on Removable Media
safeguards.description: Encrypt data on removable media.

safeguards.title: Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit
safeguards.description: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

safeguards.title: Encrypt Sensitive Data at Rest
safeguards.description: Encrypt sensitive data at rest on servers, applications, and databases containing sensitive data. Storage-layer encryption, also known as server-side encryption, meets the minimum requirement of this Safeguard. Additional encryption methods may include application-layer encryption, also known as client-side encryption, where access to the data storage device(s) does not permit access to the plaintext data.

safeguards.title: Segment Data Processing and Storage Based on Sensitivity
safeguards.description: Segment data processing and storage based on the sensitivity of the data. Do not process sensitive data on enterprise assets intended for lower sensitivity data.

safeguards.title: Deploy a Data Loss Prevention Solution
safeguards.description: Implement an automated tool, such as a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) tool, to identify all sensitive data stored, processed, or transmitted through enterprise assets, including those located onsite or at a remote service provider, and update the enterprise's sensitive data inventory.

safeguards.title: Log Sensitive Data Access
safeguards.description: Log sensitive data access, including modification and disposal.

controls.title: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.description: Establish and maintain the secure configuration of enterprise assets (end-user devices, including portable and mobile; network devices; non-computing/IoT devices; and servers) and software (operating systems and applications).

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process for enterprise assets (end-user devices, including portable and mobile; network devices; non-computing/IoT devices; and servers) and software (operating systems and applications). Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process for Network Infrastructure
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process for network devices. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Configure Automatic Session Locking on Enterprise Assets
safeguards.description: Configure automatic session locking on enterprise assets after a defined period of inactivity. For general purpose operating systems, the period must not exceed 15 minutes. For mobile end-user devices, the period must not exceed 2 minutes.

safeguards.title: Implement and Manage a Firewall on Servers
safeguards.description: Implement and manage a firewall on servers, where supported. Example implementations include a virtual firewall, operating system firewall, or a third-party firewall agent.

safeguards.title: Implement and Manage a Firewall on End-User Devices
safeguards.description: Implement and manage a host-based firewall or port-filtering tool on end-user devices, with a default-deny rule that drops all traffic except those services and ports that are explicitly allowed.

safeguards.title: Securely Manage Enterprise Assets and Software
safeguards.description: Securely manage enterprise assets and software. Example implementations include managing configuration through version-controlled-infrastructure-as-code and accessing administrative interfaces over secure network protocols, such as Secure Shell (SSH) and Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS). Do not use insecure management protocols, such as Telnet (Teletype Network) and HTTP, unless operationally essential.

safeguards.title: Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets and Software
safeguards.description: Manage default accounts on enterprise assets and software, such as root, administrator, and other pre-configured vendor accounts. Example implementations can include: disabling default accounts or making them unusable.

safeguards.title: Uninstall or Disable Unnecessary Services on Enterprise Assets and Software
safeguards.description: Uninstall or disable unnecessary services on enterprise assets and software, such as an unused file sharing service, web application module, or service function.

safeguards.title: Configure Trusted DNS Servers on Enterprise Assets
safeguards.description: Configure trusted DNS servers on enterprise assets. Example implementations include: configuring assets to use enterprise-controlled DNS servers and/or reputable externally accessible DNS servers.

safeguards.title: Enforce Automatic Device Lockout on Portable End-User Devices
safeguards.description: Enforce automatic device lockout following a predetermined threshold of local failed authentication attempts on portable end-user devices, where supported. For laptops, do not allow more than 20 failed authentication attempts; for tablets and smartphones, no more than 10 failed authentication attempts. Example implementations include Microsoft® InTune Device Compliance Policy and Apple® Configuration Profile.

safeguards.title: Enforce Remote Wipe Capability on Portable End-User Devices
safeguards.description: Remotely wipe enterprise assets on portable end-user devices when deemed appropriate such as lost or stolen devices. Example implementations include Microsoft® InTune and Apple® Business Manager.

safeguards.title: Separate Enterprise Workspaces on Mobile End-User Devices
safeguards.description: Ensure separate enterprise workspaces are used on mobile end-user devices, where supported. Example implementations include Microsoft® Intune and Apple® Business Manager.

controls.title: Account Management
controls.description: Use processes and tools to assign and manage authorization to credentials for user accounts, including administrator accounts, as well as service accounts, to enterprise assets and software.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of all accounts managed in the enterprise. The inventory must include both user and administrator accounts. The inventory, at a minimum, should contain the person's name, username, start/stop dates, and department. Validate that all active accounts are authorized, on a recurring schedule at a minimum quarterly, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Use Unique Passwords
safeguards.description: Use unique passwords for all enterprise assets. Best practice implementation includes, at a minimum, an 8-character password for accounts using MFA and a 14-character password for accounts not using MFA.

safeguards.title: Disable Dormant Accounts
safeguards.description: Delete or disable any dormant accounts after a period of 45 days of inactivity, where supported.

safeguards.title: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
safeguards.description: Restrict administrator privileges to dedicated administrator accounts on enterprise assets. Conduct general computing activities, such as internet browsing, email, and productivity suite use, from the user's primary, non-privileged account.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Accounts
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of service accounts. The inventory, at a minimum, must contain department owner, review date, and purpose. Perform service account reviews to validate that all active accounts are authorized, on a recurring schedule at a minimum quarterly, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Centralize Account Management
safeguards.description: Centralize account management through a directory or identity service.

controls.title: Access Control Management
controls.description: Use processes and tools to create, assign, manage, and revoke access credentials and privileges for user, administrator, and service/application accounts for enterprise assets and software.

safeguards.title: Establish an Access Granting Process
safeguards.description: Establish and follow a process, preferably automated, for granting access to enterprise assets upon new hire, rights grant, or role change of a user.

safeguards.title: Establish an Access Revoking Process
safeguards.description: Establish and follow a process, preferably automated, for revoking access to enterprise assets, through disabling accounts immediately upon termination, rights revocation, or role change of a user. Disabling accounts, instead of deleting accounts, may be necessary to preserve audit trails.

safeguards.title: Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
safeguards.description: Require all externally-exposed enterprise or third-party applications to enforce MFA, where supported. Enforcing MFA through a directory service or SSO provider is a satisfactory implementation of this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Require MFA for Remote Network Access
safeguards.description: Require MFA for remote network access.

safeguards.title: Require MFA for Administrative Access
safeguards.description: Require MFA for all administrative access accounts, where supported, on all enterprise assets, whether managed on-site or through a third-party provider.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Authentication and Authorization Systems
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of the enterprise's authentication and authorization systems, including those hosted on-site or at a remote service provider. Review the inventory, at a minimum, annually.

safeguards.title: Centralize Access Control
safeguards.description: Centralize access control for all enterprise assets through a directory service or SSO provider, where supported.

safeguards.title: Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
safeguards.description: Define and maintain role-based access control, through determining and documenting the access rights necessary for each role within the enterprise to successfully carry out its assigned duties. Perform access control reviews of enterprise assets to validate that all privileges are authorized, on a recurring schedule, at a minimum once every 12 months.

controls.title: Continuous Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Develop a plan to continuously assess and track vulnerabilities on all enterprise assets within the enterprise's infrastructure, in order to remediate, and minimize, the window of opportunity for attackers. Monitor public and private industry sources for new threat and vulnerability information.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Vulnerability Management Process
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a documented vulnerability management process for enterprise assets. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Remediation Process
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a risk-based remediation strategy documented in a remediation process, with monthly, or more frequent, reviews.

safeguards.title: Perform Automated Operating System Patch Management
safeguards.description: Perform operating system updates on enterprise assets through automated patch management on a monthly basis, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Perform Automated Application Patch Management
safeguards.description: Perform application updates on enterprise assets through automated patch management on a monthly basis, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Internal Enterprise Assets
safeguards.description: Perform automated vulnerability scans of internal enterprise assets on a quarterly basis, or more frequently. Conduct both authenticated and unauthenticated scans, using a SCAP-compliant vulnerability scanning tool.

safeguards.title: Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Externally-Exposed Enterprise Assets
safeguards.description: Perform automated vulnerability scans of externally-exposed enterprise assets using a SCAP-compliant vulnerability scanning tool. Perform scans on a monthly basis, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Remediate Detected Vulnerabilities
safeguards.description: Remediate detected vulnerabilities in software through processes and tooling on a monthly basis, or more frequently, based on the remediation process.

controls.title: Audit Log Management
controls.description: Collect, alert, review, and retain audit logs of events that could help detect, understand, or recover from an attack.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain an Audit Log Management Process
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an audit log management process that defines the enterprise's logging requirements. At a minimum, address the collection, review, and retention of audit logs for enterprise assets. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Collect Audit Logs
safeguards.description: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.

safeguards.title: Ensure Adequate Audit Log Storage
safeguards.description: Ensure that logging destinations maintain adequate storage to comply with the enterprise's audit log management process.

safeguards.title: Standardize Time Synchronization
safeguards.description: Standardize time synchronization. Configure at least two synchronized time sources across enterprise assets, where supported.

safeguards.title: Collect Detailed Audit Logs
safeguards.description: Configure detailed audit logging for enterprise assets containing sensitive data. Include event source, date, username, timestamp, source addresses, destination addresses, and other useful elements that could assist in a forensic investigation.

safeguards.title: Collect DNS Query Audit Logs
safeguards.description: Collect DNS query audit logs on enterprise assets, where appropriate and supported.

safeguards.title: Collect URL Request Audit Logs
safeguards.description: Collect URL request audit logs on enterprise assets, where appropriate and supported.

safeguards.title: Collect Command-Line Audit Logs
safeguards.description: Collect command-line audit logs. Example implementations include collecting audit logs from PowerShell®, BASH™, and remote administrative terminals.

safeguards.title: Centralize Audit Logs
safeguards.description: Centralize, to the extent possible, audit log collection and retention across enterprise assets.

safeguards.title: Retain Audit Logs
safeguards.description: Retain audit logs across enterprise assets for a minimum of 90 days.

safeguards.title: Conduct Audit Log Reviews
safeguards.description: Conduct reviews of audit logs to detect anomalies or abnormal events that could indicate a potential threat. Conduct reviews on a weekly basis, or more frequently.

safeguards.title: Collect Service Provider Logs
safeguards.description: Collect service provider logs, where supported. Example implementations include collecting authentication and authorization events, data creation and disposal events, user management events, and network flow records from service providers.

controls.title: Email and Web Browser Protections
controls.description: Improve protections and detections of threats from email and web vectors, as these are opportunities for attackers to manipulate human behavior through direct engagement.

safeguards.title: Ensure Use of Only Fully Supported Browsers and Email Clients
safeguards.description: Ensure only fully supported browsers and email clients are allowed to execute in the enterprise, only using the latest version of browsers and email clients provided through the vendor.

safeguards.title: Use DNS Filtering Services
safeguards.description: Use DNS filtering services on all enterprise assets to block access to known malicious domains.

safeguards.title: Maintain and Enforce Network-Based URL Filters
safeguards.description: Enforce and update network-based URL filters to limit an enterprise asset from connecting to potentially malicious or unapproved websites. Example implementations include category-based filtering, reputation-based filtering, or through the use of block lists. Enforce filters for all enterprise assets.

safeguards.title: Restrict Unnecessary or Unauthorized Browser and Email Client Extensions
safeguards.description: Restrict, either through uninstalling or disabling, any unauthorized or unnecessary browser or email client plugins, extensions, and add-on applications.

safeguards.title: Implement DMARC
safeguards.description: To lower the chance of spoofed or modified emails from valid domains, implement DMARC policy and verification, starting with implementing the Sender Policy Framework (SPF) and the DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) standards.

safeguards.title: Block Unnecessary File Types
safeguards.description: Block unnecessary file types attempting to enter the enterprise's email gateway.

safeguards.title: Deploy and Maintain Email Server Anti-Malware Protections
safeguards.description: Deploy and maintain email server anti-malware protections, such as attachment scanning and/or sandboxing.

controls.title: Malware Defenses
controls.description: Prevent or control the installation, spread, and execution of malicious applications, code, or scripts on enterprise assets.

safeguards.title: Deploy and Maintain Anti-Malware Software
safeguards.description: Deploy and maintain anti-malware software on all enterprise assets.

safeguards.title: Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Signature Updates
safeguards.description: Configure automatic updates for anti-malware signature files on all enterprise assets.

safeguards.title: Disable Autorun and Autoplay for Removable Media
safeguards.description: Disable autorun and autoplay auto-execute functionality for removable media.

safeguards.title: Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Scanning of Removable Media
safeguards.description: Configure anti-malware software to automatically scan removable media.

safeguards.title: Enable Anti-Exploitation Features
safeguards.description: Enable anti-exploitation features on enterprise assets and software, where possible, such as Microsoft® Data Execution Prevention (DEP), Windows® Defender Exploit Guard (WDEG), or Apple® System Integrity Protection (SIP) and Gatekeeper™.

safeguards.title: Centrally Manage Anti-Malware Software
safeguards.description: Centrally manage anti-malware software.

safeguards.title: Use Behavior-Based Anti-Malware Software
safeguards.description: Use behavior-based anti-malware software.

controls.title: Data Recovery
controls.description: Establish and maintain data recovery practices sufficient to restore in-scope enterprise assets to a pre-incident and trusted state.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a data recovery process. In the process, address the scope of data recovery activities, recovery prioritization, and the security of backup data. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Perform Automated Backups
safeguards.description: Perform automated backups of in-scope enterprise assets. Run backups weekly, or more frequently, based on the sensitivity of the data.

safeguards.title: Protect Recovery Data
safeguards.description: Protect recovery data with equivalent controls to the original data. Reference encryption or data separation, based on technology.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain an Isolated Instance of Recovery Data
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an isolated instance of recovery data. Example implementations include, version controlling backup destinations through offline, cloud, or off-site systems or services.

safeguards.title: Test Data Recovery
safeguards.description: Test backup recovery quarterly, or more frequently, for a sampling of in-scope enterprise assets.

controls.title: Network Infrastructure Management
controls.description: Establish, implement, and actively manage (track, report, correct) network devices, in order to prevent attackers from exploiting vulnerable network services and access points.

safeguards.title: Ensure Network Infrastructure is Up-to-Date
safeguards.description: Ensure network infrastructure is kept up-to-date. Example implementations include running the latest stable release of software and/or using currently supported network-as-a-service (NaaS) offerings. Review software versions monthly, or more frequently, to verify software support.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Network Architecture
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a secure network architecture. A secure network architecture must address segmentation, least privilege, and availability, at a minimum.

safeguards.title: Securely Manage Network Infrastructure
safeguards.description: Securely manage network infrastructure. Example implementations include version-controlled-infrastructure-as-code, and the use of secure network protocols, such as SSH and HTTPS.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain Architecture Diagram(s)
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain architecture diagram(s) and/or other network system documentation. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Centralize Network Authentication, Authorization, and Auditing (AAA)
safeguards.description: Centralize network AAA.

safeguards.title: Use of Secure Network Management and Communication Protocols
safeguards.description: Use secure network management and communication protocols (e.g., 802.1X, Wi-Fi Protected Access 2 (WPA2) Enterprise or greater).

safeguards.title: Ensure Remote Devices Utilize a VPN and are Connecting to an Enterprise's AAA Infrastructure
safeguards.description: Require users to authenticate to enterprise-managed VPN and authentication infrastructure prior to accessing enterprise resources on end-user devices.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain Dedicated Computing Resources for All Administrative Work
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain dedicated computing resources, either physically or logically separated, for all administrative tasks or tasks requiring administrative access. The computing resources should be segmented from the enterprise's primary network and not be allowed internet access.

controls.title: Network Monitoring and Defense
controls.description: Operate processes and tooling to establish and maintain comprehensive network monitoring and defense against security threats across the enterprise's network infrastructure and user base.

safeguards.title: Centralize Security Event Alerting
safeguards.description: Centralize security event alerting across enterprise assets for log correlation and analysis. Best practice implementation requires the use of a SIEM, which includes vendor-defined event correlation alerts. A log analytics platform configured with security-relevant correlation alerts also satisfies this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Detection Solution
safeguards.description: Deploy a host-based intrusion detection solution on enterprise assets, where appropriate and/or supported.

safeguards.title: Deploy a Network Intrusion Detection Solution
safeguards.description: Deploy a network intrusion detection solution on enterprise assets, where appropriate. Example implementations include the use of a Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) or equivalent cloud service provider (CSP) service.

safeguards.title: Perform Traffic Filtering Between Network Segments
safeguards.description: Perform traffic filtering between network segments, where appropriate.

safeguards.title: Manage Access Control for Remote Assets
safeguards.description: Manage access control for assets remotely connecting to enterprise resources. Determine amount of access to enterprise resources based on: up-to-date anti-malware software installed, configuration compliance with the enterprise's secure configuration process and Secure Network Management and Communications Protocols Safeguard 12.6, and ensure the operating system and applications are up-to-date.

safeguards.title: Collect Network Traffic Flow Logs
safeguards.description: Collect network traffic flow logs and/or network traffic to review and alert upon from network devices.

safeguards.title: Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Prevention Solution
safeguards.description: Deploy a host-based intrusion prevention solution on enterprise assets, where appropriate and/or supported. Example implementations include use of an Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) client.

safeguards.title: Deploy a Network Intrusion Prevention Solution
safeguards.description: Deploy a network intrusion prevention solution, where appropriate. Example implementations include the use of a Network Intrusion Prevention System (NIPS) or equivalent CSP service.

safeguards.title: Deploy Port-Level Access Control
safeguards.description: Deploy port-level access control. Port-level access control utilizes 802.1x, or similar network access control protocols, such as certificates, and may incorporate user and/or device authentication.

safeguards.title: Perform Application Layer Filtering
safeguards.description: Perform application layer filtering. Example implementations include a filtering proxy, application layer firewall, or gateway.

safeguards.title: Tune Security Event Alerting Thresholds
safeguards.description: Tune security event alerting thresholds monthly, or more frequently.

controls.title: Security Awareness and Skills Training
controls.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness program to influence behavior among the workforce to be security conscious and properly skilled to reduce cybersecurity risks to the enterprise.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Security Awareness Program
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness program. The purpose of a security awareness program is to educate the enterprise's workforce on how to interact with enterprise assets and data in a secure manner. Conduct training at hire and, at a minimum, annually. Review and update content annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Train Workforce Members to Recognize Social Engineering Attacks
safeguards.description: Train workforce members to recognize social engineering attacks, such as phishing, pre-texting, and tailgating.

safeguards.title: Train Workforce Members on Authentication Best Practices
safeguards.description: Train workforce members on authentication best practices. Example topics include MFA, password composition, and credential management.

safeguards.title: Train Workforce on Data Handling Best Practices
safeguards.description: Train workforce members on how to identify and properly store, transfer, archive, and destroy sensitive data. This also includes training workforce members on clear screen and desk best practices, such as locking their screen when they step away from their enterprise asset and properly securing sensitive printed materials.

safeguards.title: Train Workforce Members on Causes of Unintentional Data Exposure
safeguards.description: Train workforce members to be aware of causes for unintentional data exposure. Example topics include mis-delivery of sensitive data, losing a portable end-user device, or publishing data to unintended audiences.

safeguards.title: Train Workforce Members on Recognizing and Reporting Security Incidents
safeguards.description: Train workforce members to be able to recognize a potential incident and be able to report such an incident.

safeguards.title: Train Workforce on How to Identify and Report if Their Enterprise Assets are Missing Security Updates
safeguards.description: Train workforce to understand how to verify and report out-of-date software patches or any failures in automated processes and tools. Part of this training should include notifying IT personnel of any failures in automated processes and tools.

safeguards.title: Train Workforce on the Dangers of Connecting to and Transmitting Enterprise Data Over Insecure Networks
safeguards.description: Train workforce members on the dangers of connecting to and transmitting data over insecure networks for enterprise activities. If the enterprise has remote workers, training must include guidance to ensure that all users securely configure their home network infrastructure.

safeguards.title: Conduct Role-Specific Security Awareness and Skills Training
safeguards.description: Conduct role-specific security awareness and skills training. Example implementations include secure system administration courses for IT professionals, secure coding training for software developers, and social engineering awareness training for high-profile roles.

controls.title: Service Provider Management
controls.description: Develop a process to evaluate service providers who hold sensitive data, or are responsible for an enterprise's critical IT platforms or processes, to ensure these providers are protecting those platforms and data appropriately.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Providers
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of service providers. The inventory is to list all known service providers, include classification(s), and designate an enterprise contact for each service provider. Review and update the inventory annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Service Provider Management Policy
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a service provider management policy. Ensure the policy addresses the classification, inventory, assessment, monitoring, and decommissioning of service providers. Review and update the policy annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Classify Service Providers
safeguards.description: Classify service providers. Classification considerations may include one or more characteristics, such as data sensitivity, data volume, availability requirements, applicable regulations, inherent risk, and mitigated risk. Update and review classifications annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Ensure Service Provider Contracts Include Security Requirements
safeguards.description: Ensure service provider contracts include security requirements. Example requirements may include minimum security program requirements, security incident and/or data breach notification and response requirements, data encryption requirements, and data disposal requirements.

safeguards.title: Assess Service Providers
safeguards.description: Assess service providers consistent with the enterprise's service provider management policy. Assessment scope may vary based on classification(s), and may include review of standardized assessment reports, such as Service Organization Control 2 (SOC 2) and Payment Card Industry (PCI) Attestation of Compliance (AoC), customized questionnaires, or other appropriately rigorous processes. Reassess service providers annually, at a minimum, or with new and renewed contracts.

safeguards.title: Monitor Service Providers
safeguards.description: Monitor service providers consistent with the enterprise's service provider management policy. Monitoring may include periodic reassessment of service provider compliance, tracking service provider release notes, and dark web monitoring.

safeguards.title: Securely Decommission Service Providers
safeguards.description: Securely decommission service providers. Example considerations include migrating data to another service provider, destroying data at the old service provider, and removing enterprise permissions from the old service provider.

controls.title: Application Software Security
controls.description: Manage the security life cycle of in-house developed, hosted, or acquired software to prevent, detect, and remediate security weaknesses before they can impact the enterprise.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Application Development Process
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a secure application development process. In the process, address such items as: secure application design standards, secure coding practices, developer training, vulnerability management, security of third-party code, and application security testing procedures. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Process to Accept and Address Software Vulnerabilities
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a process to accept and address reports of software vulnerabilities, including providing a means for external entities to report. The process is to include such items as: a vulnerability handling policy that identifies reporting and decision processes, responsible disclosure activities, and a process for reacting to reports including the timing for remediation. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Perform Root Cause Analysis on Security Vulnerabilities
safeguards.description: Perform root cause analysis on security vulnerabilities. When reviewing vulnerabilities, root cause analysis is the task of evaluating underlying reasons for the occurrence of the vulnerability. When performing root cause analysis, enterprises may group related vulnerabilities together and perform a single root cause analysis for a group of related vulnerabilities.

safeguards.title: Establish and Manage an Inventory of Third-Party Software Components
safeguards.description: Establish and manage an updated inventory of third-party components used in development, often referred to as a Bill of Materials, as well as components slated for future use. This inventory is to include any risks that each third-party component could pose. Evaluate the list at least monthly to identify any changes or updates to these third-party components.

safeguards.title: Use Up-to-Date and Trusted Third-Party Software Components
safeguards.description: Use up-to-date and trusted third-party software components. When possible, choose established and proven frameworks and libraries that provide adequate security. Acquire these components from trusted sources, use those that are actively maintained, and ensure a high level of security quality is maintained when considering components that are independently developed.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Severity Rating System and Process for Application Vulnerabilities
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a severity rating system and process for application vulnerabilities that facilitates prioritizing the order in which discovered vulnerabilities are fixed. This process includes setting a minimum level of security acceptability for releasing code or applications, and requiring that peer review be performed before releasing code or applications.

safeguards.title: Use Standard Hardening Configuration Templates for Application Infrastructure
safeguards.description: Use standard, industry-recommended hardening configuration templates for application infrastructure components. This includes underlying servers, databases, and web servers, and applies to cloud containers, Platform as a Service (PaaS) components, and SaaS components. Do not allow in-house developed software to weaken industry-recommended hardening configurations.

safeguards.title: Separate Production and Non-Production Systems
safeguards.description: Maintain separate environments for production and non-production systems.

safeguards.title: Train Developers in Application Security Concepts and Secure Coding
safeguards.description: Ensure that all software development personnel receive training in writing secure code for their specific development environment and responsibilities. Training can include general security principles and application security standard practices. Conduct training at least annually and update content to address relevant threats and technical approaches.

safeguards.title: Apply Secure Design Principles in Application Architectures
safeguards.description: Apply secure design principles in application architectures. Example principles include the concept of least privilege, defense-in-depth, fail secure, do not trust services, separation of privilege, avoid security by obscurity, and simplicity of design.

safeguards.title: Leverage Vetted Modules or Services for Application Security Components
safeguards.description: Leverage vetted modules or services for application security components, such as identity management, encryption, and auditing and logging. Using platform features in critical security functions will reduce developers' workload and minimize the likelihood of design or implementation errors. Example implementations include the use of a single sign-on service, a standard encryption library, and an established logging service.

safeguards.title: Implement Code-Level Security Checks
safeguards.description: Apply static and dynamic analysis tools to verify that secure coding practices are being adhered to for internally developed software.

safeguards.title: Conduct Application Penetration Testing
safeguards.description: Conduct application penetration testing. For critical applications, authenticated penetration testing is preferred. Penetration testing must include, at a minimum, OWASP Top 10 issues. Use a reputable penetration testing service provider or a pentest product. Perform application penetration testing periodically, at a minimum annually.

safeguards.title: Conduct Threat Modeling
safeguards.description: Conduct threat modeling. Use a repeatable process to identify and address potential weaknesses in applications. Threat modeling should include the consideration of human threat sources (adversaries), system-level weaknesses, technical impacts, and business impacts. The process used should produce an prioritized list of potential weaknesses in the form of threats.

controls.title: Incident Response Management
controls.description: Establish a program to develop and maintain an incident response capability (e.g., policies, plans, procedures, defined roles, training, and communications) to prepare, detect, and quickly respond to an attack.

safeguards.title: Designate Personnel to Manage Incident Handling
safeguards.description: Designate one key person, and at least one backup, who will manage the enterprise's incident handling process. Management personnel are responsible for the coordination and documentation of incident response and recovery efforts and can consist of employees or third-party contractors.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain contact information for parties that need to be informed of security incidents. Contacts may include internal staff, third-party vendors, law enforcement, cyber insurance providers, relevant government agencies, Information Sharing and Analysis Center (ISAC) partners, or other stakeholders. Verify contacts annually to ensure that information is up-to-date.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain an Enterprise Process for Reporting Incidents
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an enterprise process for the workforce to report security incidents. The process includes reporting timeframe, personnel to report to, mechanism for reporting, and the minimum information to be reported. Ensure the process is publicly available to all of the workforce. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain an incident response process that addresses roles and responsibilities, compliance requirements, and a communication plan. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Assign Key Roles and Responsibilities
safeguards.description: Assign key roles and responsibilities for incident response, including staff from legal, IT, information security, facilities, public relations, human resources, incident responders, and analysts, as applicable. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Define Mechanisms for Communicating During Incident Response
safeguards.description: Determine which primary and secondary mechanisms will be used to communicate and report during a security incident. Mechanisms can include phone calls, emails, or letters. Keep in mind that certain mechanisms, such as emails, can be affected during a security incident. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

safeguards.title: Conduct Routine Incident Response Exercises
safeguards.description: Plan and conduct routine incident response exercises and scenarios for key personnel involved in the incident response process to prepare for responding to real-world incidents. Exercises need to test communication channels, decision making, and workflows. Conduct testing on an annual basis, at a minimum.

safeguards.title: Conduct Post-Incident Reviews
safeguards.description: Conduct post-incident reviews. Post-incident reviews help prevent incident recurrence through identifying lessons learned and follow-up action.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain Security Incident Thresholds
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain security incident thresholds, including, at a minimum, differentiating between an incident and an event. Examples can include: unexpected downtime, unauthorized software installation, unauthorized access to systems or data, loss or theft of devices, ransomware attacks, and data breaches.

controls.title: Penetration Testing
controls.description: Test the effectiveness and resiliency of enterprise assets through identifying and exploiting weaknesses in controls (people, processes, and technology), and simulating the objectives and actions of an attacker.

safeguards.title: Establish and Maintain a Penetration Testing Program
safeguards.description: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program appropriate to the size, complexity, and maturity of the enterprise. A penetration testing program includes a policy that defines the scope, target systems, and testing methods that are consistent with the enterprise's overall risk tolerance and objectives.

safeguards.title: Perform Periodic External Penetration Tests
safeguards.description: Perform periodic external penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually. External penetration testing must include enterprise and environmental reconnaissance to detect exploitable information. Penetration testing requires specialized skills and experience and must be conducted through a qualified party. The testing may be clear box or opaque box.

safeguards.title: Remediate Penetration Test Findings
safeguards.description: Remediate penetration test findings based on the enterprise's policy for remediation scope and prioritization.

safeguards.title: Validate Security Measures
safeguards.description: Validate security measures after each penetration test. If deemed necessary, modify rulesets and capabilities to detect the techniques used during testing.

safeguards.title: Perform Periodic Internal Penetration Tests
safeguards.description: Perform periodic internal penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually. The testing may be clear box or opaque box.
